task_completion.description: Verify the agent opened the correct logistics dataset.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a spreadsheet application (like Excel or LibreOffice Calc) open with 'logistics_audit_q3.xlsx' visible in the title bar.

task_completion.description: Verify the 'Rebate Amount' column is created and populated on the 'Shipments' sheet.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a new column named 'Rebate Amount' in the 'Shipments' sheet. The cells should contain numerical values (0 for on-time shipments, and positive values for late shipments). The formula bar should ideally show logic comparing Actual Days to Scheduled Days and multiplying Sales Amount by the SLA percentage.

task_completion.description: Verify red conditional formatting is applied to Rebate Amounts greater than $50.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the 'Rebate Amount' column. Cells with values strictly greater than 50 must be highlighted in red (either red text or red background fill). Cells with values of 50 or less, including 0, must not have this red formatting.

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of the 'Audit_Summary' sheet.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a sheet tab named 'Audit_Summary' at the bottom of the spreadsheet window.

task_completion.description: Verify the summary sheet displays the total rebate amount for each Shipping Mode.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the 'Audit_Summary' sheet. There should be a table or PivotTable listing the Shipping Modes (Same Day, First Class, Second Class, Standard Class) and their corresponding total summed Rebate Amounts.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the spreadsheet application's graphical user interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent clicking through the spreadsheet application's ribbons, menus, and dialog boxes (e.g., Conditional Formatting rules, PivotTable creation) rather than executing a Python/pandas script in a terminal.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used formulas or built-in tools rather than hardcoding answers.
integrity.visual_evidence: When a calculated cell is selected, the formula bar should show a formula (e.g., =IF(...), =VLOOKUP(...)) or the summary should be a proper PivotTable/SUMIFS function, not manually typed static numbers.

integrity.description: Verify conditional formatting was applied via rules, not manual cell coloring.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the Conditional Formatting dialog box being opened and configured with the '> 50' rule, rather than the agent manually selecting individual cells and changing their color.

privileged_info_for_vlm: File: logistics_audit_q3.xlsx. Sheets: 'Shipments' (data), 'SLA_Policy' (rules), 'Audit_Summary' (created by agent). Columns: Order ID, Shipping Mode, Sales Amount, Scheduled Days, Actual Days, Rebate Amount. Rebate calculation logic: If Actual Days > Scheduled Days, Rebate = Sales Amount * Rebate %; else 0. Rebate percentages by mode: Same Day (100% or 1.0), First Class (50% or 0.5), Second Class (10% or 0.1), Standard Class (0% or 0.0). Conditional formatting: Red formatting strictly for Rebate Amount > 50.
